name: Thomas Wolfe
abstract: Thomas Clayton Wolfe (October 3, 1900 – September 15, 1938) was an American novelist and short story writer. He is known largely for his first novel, Look Homeward, Angel (1929), and for the short fiction that appeared during the last years of his life.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: I would draw your attention to the possibility of making such an analogy, for instance, between Bix and Thomas Wolfe, who fundamentally were from the same origins and developing the same kinds of attitudes and reactions, using similar symbols, although each in a different idiom.
